{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q841085]] Wikidata name: 21st day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|November 21|qq=November 21}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月廿一|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 21일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 21''' is the 21st day of the month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senshū|qid=Q96759839}}{{efn|ja|先勝|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 3rd year（[[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:887|887]] [[:en:December 9|December 9]]） – The Daijō-daijin and Fujiwara Mototsune were appointed as {{ill|Kanpaku|ja|関白|fr|Kanpaku|ru|Кампаку|sv|Kampaku|uk|Кампаку|zh|關白|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpaku|qq=|1=kampaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1199966}}, becoming the first commoner Kanpaku. * [[:en:Tengyō|Tenkei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:940|940]] [[:en:January 3|January 3]]） – Taira no Masakado attacked the Hitachi provincial government, seizing official seals and capturing Hitachi-no-kasuke and Fujiwara Noriaki. * [[:en:承徳|承徳]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1097|1097]] [[:en:December 27|December 27]]） – Due to a celestial event, the era name was changed from Eichō to Shōtoku. * [[乾元 (日本)|乾元]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1302|1302]] [[:en:December 10|December 10]]） – The era name was changed from Seian to Kangen for the accession of Emperor Go-Nijō. * [[:en:安永|安永]] 8th year（[[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1779|1779]] [[:en:December 28|December 28]]） – Hiraga Gennai was imprisoned on suspicion of accidentally killing two of his disciples. * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] [[:en:December 19|December 19]]） – The Tokugawa shogunate signed the "Yokohama Residence Memorandum" with Britain, the United States, France, and the Netherlands.
== Births == * [[:en:En'ō|En'ō]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1239|1239]] [[:en:December 17|December 17]]） – Fujiwara Yoritsugu, the 5th shogun of the Kamakura shogunate (d. [[:en:1256|1256]]). * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 18th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1733|1733]] [[:en:December 26|December 26]]） – Makino Sadanaga, a politician (d. [[:en:1796|1796]]). * {{ill|Enkyō|1=Enkyō|2=ja|3=延享|lt=Enkyō|ja|Enkyō|1=Enkyō (Edo period)|2=ja|3=延享|lt=Enkyō|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1183728}} 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1747|1747]] [[:en:December 17|December 17]]） – Arakida Hisao, a kokugaku scholar (d. [[:en:1804|1804]]). * [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1863|1863]] [[:en:December 31|December 31]]） – Nagase Tomorō, the founder of Kao (d. [[:en:1911|1911]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:January 11|January 11]]） – Taoka Reiun, a writer and critic (d. [[:en:1912|1912]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei]] 13th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1481|1481]] [[:en:December 12|December 12]]） – Ikkyū Sōjun, a Zen monk (born [[:en:1394|1394]]). * [[:en:Man'en|Man'en]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1861|1861]] [[:en:January 1|January 1]]） – Asaka Gensai, a Confucian scholar ({{ill|Ru (Confucian scholar)|ja|儒学者|zh|儒生|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ru (Confucian scholar)|qq=}}) (born [[:en:1791|1791]]).

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 21 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月21日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 21일]][[zh_min_nan:11 goe̍h 21 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:十一月廿一]][[zh:十一月廿一]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q841085]] Wikidata name: 21st day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|November 21|qq=November 21}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月廿一|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 21일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 21''' is the 21st day of the month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senshū|qid=Q96759839}}{{efn|ja|先勝|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 3rd year（[[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:887|887]] [[:en:December 9|December 9]]） – The Daijō-daijin and Fujiwara Mototsune were appointed as {{ill|Kanpaku|ja|関白|fr|Kanpaku|ru|Кампаку|sv|Kampaku|uk|Кампаку|zh|關白|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpaku|qq=|1=kampaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1199966}}, becoming the first commoner Kanpaku. * [[:en:Tengyō|Tenkei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:940|940]] [[:en:January 3|January 3]]） – Taira no Masakado attacked the Hitachi provincial government, seizing official seals and capturing Hitachi-no-kasuke and Fujiwara Noriaki. * [[:en:承徳|承徳]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1097|1097]] [[:en:December 27|December 27]]） – Due to a celestial event, the era name was changed from Eichō to Shōtoku. * [[乾元 (日本)|乾元]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1302|1302]] [[:en:December 10|December 10]]） – The era name was changed from Seian to Kangen for the accession of Emperor Go-Nijō. * [[:en:安永|安永]] 8th year（[[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1779|1779]] [[:en:December 28|December 28]]） – Hiraga Gennai was imprisoned on suspicion of accidentally killing two of his disciples. * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] [[:en:December 19|December 19]]） – The Tokugawa shogunate signed the "Yokohama Residence Memorandum" with Britain, the United States, France, and the Netherlands.
== Births == * [[:en:En'ō|En'ō]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1239|1239]] [[:en:December 17|December 17]]） – Fujiwara Yoritsugu, the 5th shogun of the Kamakura shogunate (d. [[:en:1256|1256]]). * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 18th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1733|1733]] [[:en:December 26|December 26]]） – Makino Sadanaga, a politician (d. [[:en:1796|1796]]). * {{ill|Enkyō|1=Enkyō|2=ja|3=延享|lt=Enkyō|ja|Enkyō|1=Enkyō (Edo period)|2=ja|3=延享|lt=Enkyō|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1183728}} 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1747|1747]] [[:en:December 17|December 17]]） – Arakida Hisao, a kokugaku scholar (d. [[:en:1804|1804]]). * [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1863|1863]] [[:en:December 31|December 31]]） – Nagase Tomorō, the founder of Kao (d. [[:en:1911|1911]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:January 11|January 11]]） – Taoka Reiun, a writer and critic (d. [[:en:1912|1912]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei]] 13th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1481|1481]] [[:en:December 12|December 12]]） – Ikkyū Sōjun, a Zen monk (born [[:en:1394|1394]]). * [[:en:Man'en|Man'en]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1861|1861]] [[:en:January 1|January 1]]） – Asaka Gensai, a Confucian scholar ({{ill|Ru (Confucian scholar)|ja|儒学者|zh|儒生|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ru (Confucian scholar)|qq=}}) (born [[:en:1791|1791]]).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|20|11|22|10|21|12|11|1121|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 21 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月21日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 21일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 21 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月廿一]][[zh:十一月廿一]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q841085]] Wikidata name: 21st day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|November 21|qq=November 21}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月廿一|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 21일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 21''' is the 21st day of the month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senshū|qid=Q96759839}}{{efn|ja|先勝|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 3rd year（[[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:887|887]] [[:en:December 9|December 9]]） – The Daijō-daijin and Fujiwara Mototsune were appointed as {{ill|Kanpaku|ja|関白|fr|Kanpaku|ru|Кампаку|sv|Kampaku|uk|Кампаку|zh|關白|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpaku|qq=|1=kampaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1199966}}, becoming the first commoner Kanpaku. * [[:en:Tengyō|Tenkei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:940|940]] [[:en:January 3|January 3]]） – Taira no Masakado attacked the Hitachi provincial government, seizing official seals and capturing Hitachi-no-kasuke and Fujiwara Noriaki. * [[:en:承徳|承徳]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1097|1097]] [[:en:December 27|December 27]]） – Due to a celestial event, the era name was changed from Eichō to Shōtoku. * [[乾元 (日本)|乾元]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1302|1302]] [[:en:December 10|December 10]]） – The era name was changed from Seian to Kangen for the accession of Emperor Go-Nijō. * [[:en:安永|安永]] 8th year（[[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1779|1779]] [[:en:December 28|December 28]]） – Hiraga Gennai was imprisoned on suspicion of accidentally killing two of his disciples. * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] [[:en:December 19|December 19]]） – The Tokugawa shogunate signed the "Yokohama Residence Memorandum" with Britain, the United States, France, and the Netherlands.
== Births == * [[:en:En'ō|En'ō]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1239|1239]] [[:en:December 17|December 17]]） – Fujiwara Yoritsugu, the 5th shogun of the Kamakura shogunate (d. [[:en:1256|1256]]). * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 18th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1733|1733]] [[:en:December 26|December 26]]） – Makino Sadanaga, a politician (d. [[:en:1796|1796]]). * {{ill|Enkyō|1=Enkyō|2=ja|3=延享|lt=Enkyō|ja|Enkyō|1=Enkyō (Edo period)|2=ja|3=延享|lt=Enkyō|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1183728}} 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1747|1747]] [[:en:December 17|December 17]]） – Arakida Hisao, a kokugaku scholar (d. [[:en:1804|1804]]). * [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1863|1863]] [[:en:December 31|December 31]]） – Nagase Tomorō, the founder of Kao (d. [[:en:1911|1911]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:January 11|January 11]]） – Taoka Reiun, a writer and critic (d. [[:en:1912|1912]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei]] 13th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1481|1481]] [[:en:December 12|December 12]]） – Ikkyū Sōjun, a Zen monk (born [[:en:1394|1394]]). * [[:en:Man'en|Man'en]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1861|1861]] [[:en:January 1|January 1]]） – Asaka Gensai, a Confucian scholar ({{ill|Ru (Confucian scholar)|ja|儒学者|zh|儒生|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ru (Confucian scholar)|qq=}}) (born [[:en:1791|1791]]).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|20|11|22|10|21|12|11|1121|qq=}}
==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}
[[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 21 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月廿一]]
{{translated page|ja|11月21日 (旧暦)|version=60216887|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[ko:음력 11월 21일]] [[ja:11月21日 (旧暦)]] [[zh:十一月廿一]]
[[ar:November 21 (Lunisolar)]]
[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] [[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 21 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月廿一]] <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 21 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月廿一]] {{wikidata link|Q841085}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Non matching pagename on wikidata]]

Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月廿一|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 21일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 21''' is the 21st day of the month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senshū|WD=Q96759839}}{{efn|ja|先勝|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 3rd year（[[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:887|887]] [[:en:December 9|December 9]]） – The Daijō-daijin and Fujiwara Mototsune were appointed as {{ill|Kanpaku|ja|関白|fr|Kanpaku|ru|Кампаку|sv|Kampaku|uk|Кампаку|zh|關白|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpaku|1=kampaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1199966}}, becoming the first commoner Kanpaku. * [[:en:Tengyō|Tenkei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:940|940]] [[:en:January 3|January 3]]） – Taira no Masakado attacked the Hitachi provincial government, seizing official seals and capturing Hitachi-no-kasuke and Fujiwara Noriaki. * [[:en:承徳|承徳]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1097|1097]] [[:en:December 27|December 27]]） – Due to a celestial event, the era name was changed from Eichō to Shōtoku. * [[乾元 (日本)|乾元]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1302|1302]] [[:en:December 10|December 10]]） – The era name was changed from Seian to Kangen for the accession of Emperor Go-Nijō. * [[:en:安永|安永]] 8th year（[[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1779|1779]] [[:en:December 28|December 28]]） – Hiraga Gennai was imprisoned on suspicion of accidentally killing two of his disciples. * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] [[:en:December 19|December 19]]） – The Tokugawa shogunate signed the "Yokohama Residence Memorandum" with Britain, the United States, France, and the Netherlands.
== Births == * [[:en:En'ō|En'ō]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1239|1239]] [[:en:December 17|December 17]]） – Fujiwara Yoritsugu, the 5th shogun of the Kamakura shogunate (d. [[:en:1256|1256]]). * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 18th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1733|1733]] [[:en:December 26|December 26]]） – Makino Sadanaga, a politician (d. [[:en:1796|1796]]). * {{ill|Enkyō|1=Enkyō|2=ja|3=延享|lt=Enkyō|ja|Enkyō|1=Enkyō (Edo period)|2=ja|3=延享|lt=Enkyō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1183728}} 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1747|1747]] [[:en:December 17|December 17]]） – Arakida Hisao, a kokugaku scholar (d. [[:en:1804|1804]]). * [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1863|1863]] [[:en:December 31|December 31]]） – Nagase Tomorō, the founder of Kao (d. [[:en:1911|1911]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:January 11|January 11]]） – Taoka Reiun, a writer and critic (d. [[:en:1912|1912]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei]] 13th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1481|1481]] [[:en:December 12|December 12]]） – Ikkyū Sōjun, a Zen monk (born [[:en:1394|1394]]). * [[:en:Man'en|Man'en]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1861|1861]] [[:en:January 1|January 1]]） – Asaka Gensai, a Confucian scholar ({{ill|Ru (Confucian scholar)|ja|儒学者|zh|儒生|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ru (Confucian scholar)|qq=}}) (born [[:en:1791|1791]]).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|20|11|22|10|21|12|11|1121|qq=}}

Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senshū|WD=Q96759839}}{{efn|ja|先勝|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 3rd year（[[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:887|887]] [[:en:December 9|December 9]]） – The Daijō-daijin and Fujiwara Mototsune were appointed as {{ill|Kanpaku|ja|関白|fr|Kanpaku|ru|Кампаку|sv|Kampaku|uk|Кампаку|zh|關白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpaku|1=kampaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1199966}}, becoming the first commoner Kanpaku. * [[:en:Tengyō|Tenkei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:940|940]] [[:en:January 3|January 3]]） – Taira no Masakado attacked the Hitachi provincial government, seizing official seals and capturing Hitachi-no-kasuke and Fujiwara Noriaki. * [[:en:承徳|承徳]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1097|1097]] [[:en:December 27|December 27]]） – Due to a celestial event, the era name was changed from Eichō to Shōtoku. * [[乾元 (日本)|乾元]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1302|1302]] [[:en:December 10|December 10]]） – The era name was changed from Seian to Kangen for the accession of Emperor Go-Nijō. * [[:en:安永|安永]] 8th year（[[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1779|1779]] [[:en:December 28|December 28]]） – Hiraga Gennai was imprisoned on suspicion of accidentally killing two of his disciples. * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] [[:en:December 19|December 19]]） – The Tokugawa shogunate signed the "Yokohama Residence Memorandum" with Britain, the United States, France, and the Netherlands.
== Births == * [[:en:En'ō|En'ō]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1239|1239]] [[:en:December 17|December 17]]） – Fujiwara Yoritsugu, the 5th shogun of the Kamakura shogunate (d. [[:en:1256|1256]]). * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 18th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1733|1733]] [[:en:December 26|December 26]]） – Makino Sadanaga, a politician (d. [[:en:1796|1796]]). * {{ill|Enkyō|1=Enkyō|2=ja|3=延享|lt=Enkyō|ja|Enkyō|1=Enkyō (Edo period)|2=ja|3=延享|lt=Enkyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1183728}} 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1747|1747]] [[:en:December 17|December 17]]） – Arakida Hisao, a kokugaku scholar (d. [[:en:1804|1804]]). * [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1863|1863]] [[:en:December 31|December 31]]） – Nagase Tomorō, the founder of Kao (d. [[:en:1911|1911]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:January 11|January 11]]） – Taoka Reiun, a writer and critic (d. [[:en:1912|1912]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei]] 13th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1481|1481]] [[:en:December 12|December 12]]） – Ikkyū Sōjun, a Zen monk (born [[:en:1394|1394]]). * [[:en:Man'en|Man'en]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1861|1861]] [[:en:January 1|January 1]]） – Asaka Gensai, a Confucian scholar ({{ill|Ru (Confucian scholar)|ja|儒学者|zh|儒生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ru (Confucian scholar)|qq=}}) (born [[:en:1791|1791]]).

== Events == * [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 3rd year（[[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:887|887]] [[:en:December 9|December 9]]） – The Daijō-daijin and Fujiwara Mototsune were appointed as {{ill|Kanpaku|ja|関白|fr|Kanpaku|ru|Кампаку|sv|Kampaku|uk|Кампаку|zh|關白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpaku|1=kampaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1199966}}, becoming the first commoner Kanpaku. * [[:en:Tengyō|Tenkei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:940|940]] [[:en:January 3|January 3]]） – Taira no Masakado attacked the Hitachi provincial government, seizing official seals and capturing Hitachi-no-kasuke and Fujiwara Noriaki. * [[:en:承徳|承徳]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1097|1097]] [[:en:December 27|December 27]]） – Due to a celestial event, the era name was changed from Eichō to Shōtoku. * [[乾元 (日本)|乾元]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1302|1302]] [[:en:December 10|December 10]]） – The era name was changed from Seian to Kangen for the accession of Emperor Go-Nijō. * [[:en:安永|安永]] 8th year（[[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1779|1779]] [[:en:December 28|December 28]]） – Hiraga Gennai was imprisoned on suspicion of accidentally killing two of his disciples. * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] [[:en:December 19|December 19]]） – The Tokugawa shogunate signed the "Yokohama Residence Memorandum" with Britain, the United States, France, and the Netherlands.
== Births == * [[:en:En'ō|En'ō]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1239|1239]] [[:en:December 17|December 17]]） – Fujiwara Yoritsugu, the 5th shogun of the Kamakura shogunate (d. [[:en:1256|1256]]). * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 18th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1733|1733]] [[:en:December 26|December 26]]） – Makino Sadanaga, a politician (d. [[:en:1796|1796]]). * {{ill|Enkyō|1=Enkyō|2=ja|3=延享|lt=Enkyō|ja|Enkyō|1=Enkyō (Edo period)|2=ja|3=延享|lt=Enkyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1183728}} 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1747|1747]] [[:en:December 17|December 17]]） – Arakida Hisao, a kokugaku scholar (d. [[:en:1911|1911]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:January 11|January 11]]） – Taoka Reiun, a writer and critic (d. [[:en:1912|1912]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei]] 13th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1481|1481]] [[:en:December 12|December 12]]） – Ikkyū Sōjun, a Zen monk (born [[:en:1394|1394]]). * [[:en:Man'en|Man'en]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1861|1861]] [[:en:January 1|January 1]]） – Asaka Gensai, a Confucian scholar ({{ill|Ru (Confucian scholar)|ja|儒学者|zh|儒生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ru (Confucian scholar)|qq=}}) (born [[:en:1791|1791]]).

== Events == * [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 3rd year（[[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:887|887]] [[:en:December 9|December 9]]） – The Daijō-daijin and Fujiwara Mototsune were appointed as {{ill|Kanpaku|ja|関白|fr|Kanpaku|ru|Кампаку|sv|Kampaku|uk|Кампаку|zh|關白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpaku|1=kampaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1199966}}, becoming the first commoner Kanpaku. * [[:en:Tengyō|Tenkei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:940|940]] [[:en:January 3|January 3]]） – Taira no Masakado attacked the Hitachi provincial government, seizing official seals and capturing Hitachi-no-kasuke and Fujiwara Noriaki. * [[:en:承徳|承徳]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1097|1097]] [[:en:December 27|December 27]]） – Due to a celestial event, the era name was changed from Eichō to Shōtoku. * [[乾元 (日本)|乾元]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1302|1302]] [[:en:December 10|December 10]]） – The era name was changed from Seian to Kangen for the accession of Emperor Go-Nijō. * [[:en:安永|安永]] 8th year（[[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1779|1779]] [[:en:December 28|December 28]]） – Hiraga Gennai was imprisoned on suspicion of accidentally killing two of his disciples. * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] [[:en:December 19|December 19]]） – The Tokugawa shogunate signed the "Yokohama Residence Memorandum" with Britain, the United States, France, and the Netherlands.
== Births == * [[:en:En'ō|En'ō]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1239|1239]] [[:en:December 17|December 17]]） – Fujiwara Yoritsugu, the 5th shogun of the Kamakura shogunate (d. [[:en:1256|1256]]). * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 18th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1733|1733]] [[:en:December 26|December 26]]） – Makino Sadanaga, a politician (d. [[:en:1796|1796]]). * {{ill|Enkyō|1=Enkyō|2=ja|3=延享|lt=Enkyō|ja|Enkyō|1=Enkyō (Edo period)|2=ja|3=延享|lt=Enkyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1183728}} 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1747|1747]] [[:en:December 17|December 17]]） – Arakida Hisao, a kokugaku scholar (d. [[:en:1804|1804]]). * [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1863|1863]] [[:en:December 31|December 31]]） – Nagase Tomorō, the founder of Kao (d. [[:en:1911|1911]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:January 11|January 11]]） – Taoka Reiun, a writer and critic (d. [[:en:1912|1912]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei]] 13th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1481|1481]] [[:en:December 12|December 12]]） – Ikkyū Sōjun, a Zen monk (born [[:en:1394|1394]]). * [[:en:Man'en|Man'en]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1861|1861]] [[:en:January 1|January 1]]） – Asaka Gensai, a Confucian scholar ({{ill|Ru (Confucian scholar)|ja|儒学者|zh|儒生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ru (Confucian scholar)|qq=}}) (born [[:en:1791|1791]]).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|20|11|22|10|21|12|11|1121|qq=}}

Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senshū|WD=Q96759839}}{{efn|ja|先勝|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 3rd year（[[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:887|887]] [[:en:December 9|December 9]]） – The Daijō-daijin and Fujiwara Mototsune were appointed as {{ill|Kanpaku|ja|関白|fr|Kanpaku|ru|Кампаку|sv|Kampaku|uk|Кампаку|zh|關白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpaku|1=kampaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1199966}}, becoming the first commoner Kanpaku. * [[:en:Tengyō|Tenkei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:940|940]] [[:en:January 3|January 3]]） – Taira no Masakado attacked the Hitachi provincial government, seizing official seals and capturing Hitachi-no-kasuke and Fujiwara Noriaki. * [[:en:承徳|承徳]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1097|1097]] [[:en:December 27|December 27]]） – Due to a celestial event, the era name was changed from Eichō to Shōtoku. * [[乾元 (日本)|乾元]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1302|1302]] [[:en:December 10|December 10]]） – The era name was changed from Seian to Kangen for the accession of Emperor Go-Nijō. * [[:en:安永|安永]] 8th year（[[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1779|1779]] [[:en:December 28|December 28]]） – Hiraga Gennai was imprisoned on suspicion of accidentally killing two of his disciples. * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] [[:en:December 19|December 19]]） – The Tokugawa shogunate signed the "Yokohama Residence Memorandum" with Britain, the United States, France, and the Netherlands.
== Births == * [[:en:En'ō|En'ō]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1239|1239]] [[:en:December 17|December 17]]） – Fujiwara Yoritsugu, the 5th shogun of the Kamakura shogunate (d. [[:en:1256|1256]]). * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 18th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1733|1733]] [[:en:December 26|December 26]]） – Makino Sadanaga, a politician (d. [[:en:1796|1796]]). * {{ill|Enkyō|1=Enkyō|2=ja|3=延享|lt=Enkyō|ja|Enkyō|1=Enkyō (Edo period)|2=ja|3=延享|lt=Enkyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1183728}} 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1747|1747]] [[:en:December 17|December 17]]） – Arakida Hisao, a kokugaku scholar (d. [[:en:1804|1804]]). * [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1863|1863]] [[:en:December 31|December 31]]） – Nagase Tomorō, the founder of Kao (d. [[:en:1911|1911]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:January 11|January 11]]） – Taoka Reiun, a writer and critic (d. [[:en:1912|1912]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei]] 13th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1481|1481]] [[:en:December 12|December 12]]） – Ikkyū Sōjun, a Zen monk (born [[:en:1394|1394]]). * [[:en:Man'en|Man'en]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1861|1861]] [[:en:January 1|January 1]]） – Asaka Gensai, a Confucian scholar ({{ill|Ru (Confucian scholar)|ja|儒学者|zh|儒生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ru (Confucian scholar)|qq=}}) (born [[:en:1791|1791]]).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|20|11|22|10|21|12|11|1121|qq=}}

Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月廿一|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 21일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 21''' is the 21st day of the month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senshū|WD=Q96759839}}{{efn|ja|先勝|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 3rd year（[[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:887|887]] [[:en:December 9|December 9]]） – The Daijō-daijin and Fujiwara Mototsune were appointed as {{ill|Kanpaku|ja|関白|fr|Kanpaku|ru|Кампаку|sv|Kampaku|uk|Кампаку|zh|關白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpaku|1=kampaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1199966}}, becoming the first commoner Kanpaku. * [[:en:Tengyō|Tenkei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:940|940]] [[:en:January 3|January 3]]） – Taira no Masakado attacked the Hitachi provincial government, seizing official seals and capturing Hitachi-no-kasuke and Fujiwara Noriaki. * [[:en:承徳|承徳]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1097|1097]] [[:en:December 27|December 27]]） – Due to a celestial event, the era name was changed from Eichō to Shōtoku. * [[乾元 (日本)|乾元]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1302|1302]] [[:en:December 10|December 10]]） – The era name was changed from Seian to Kangen for the accession of Emperor Go-Nijō. * [[:en:安永|安永]] 8th year（[[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1779|1779]] [[:en:December 28|December 28]]） – Hiraga Gennai was imprisoned on suspicion of accidentally killing two of his disciples. * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] [[:en:December 19|December 19]]） – The Tokugawa shogunate signed the "Yokohama Residence Memorandum" with Britain, the United States, France, and the Netherlands.
== Births == * [[:en:En'ō|En'ō]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1239|1239]] [[:en:December 17|December 17]]） – Fujiwara Yoritsugu, the 5th shogun of the Kamakura shogunate (d. [[:en:1256|1256]]). * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 18th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1733|1733]] [[:en:December 26|December 26]]） – Makino Sadanaga, a politician (d. [[:en:1796|1796]]). * {{ill|Enkyō|1=Enkyō|2=ja|3=延享|lt=Enkyō|ja|Enkyō|1=Enkyō (Edo period)|2=ja|3=延享|lt=Enkyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1183728}} 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1747|1747]] [[:en:December 17|December 17]]） – Arakida Hisao, a kokugaku scholar (d. [[:en:1804|1804]]). * [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1863|1863]] [[:en:December 31|December 31]]） – Nagase Tomorō, the founder of Kao (d. [[:en:1911|1911]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:January 11|January 11]]） – Taoka Reiun, a writer and critic (d. [[:en:1912|1912]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei]] 13th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1481|1481]] [[:en:December 12|December 12]]） – Ikkyū Sōjun, a Zen monk (born [[:en:1394|1394]]). * [[:en:Man'en|Man'en]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1861|1861]] [[:en:January 1|January 1]]） – Asaka Gensai, a Confucian scholar ({{ill|Ru (Confucian scholar)|ja|儒学者|zh|儒生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ru (Confucian scholar)|qq=}}) (born [[:en:1791|1791]]).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|20|11|22|10|21|12|11|1121|qq=}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "zh_min_nan" to "zh-min-nan"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 21 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月21日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 21일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 21 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:十一月廿一]][[zh:十一月廿一]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 21 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月21日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 21일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 21 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:十一月廿一]][[zh:十一月廿一]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 21 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月21日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 21일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 21 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月廿一]][[zh:十一月廿一]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q841085]] Wikidata name: 21st day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|November 21|qq=November 21}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月廿一|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 21일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 21''' is the 21st day of the month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senshū|WD=Q96759839}}{{efn|ja|先勝|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 3rd year（[[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:887|887]] [[:en:December 9|December 9]]） – The Daijō-daijin and Fujiwara Mototsune were appointed as {{ill|Kanpaku|ja|関白|fr|Kanpaku|ru|Кампаку|sv|Kampaku|uk|Кампаку|zh|關白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpaku|1=kampaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1199966}}, becoming the first commoner Kanpaku. * [[:en:Tengyō|Tenkei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:940|940]] [[:en:January 3|January 3]]） – Taira no Masakado attacked the Hitachi provincial government, seizing official seals and capturing Hitachi-no-kasuke and Fujiwara Noriaki. * [[:en:承徳|承徳]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1097|1097]] [[:en:December 27|December 27]]） – Due to a celestial event, the era name was changed from Eichō to Shōtoku. * [[乾元 (日本)|乾元]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1302|1302]] [[:en:December 10|December 10]]） – The era name was changed from Seian to Kangen for the accession of Emperor Go-Nijō. * [[:en:安永|安永]] 8th year（[[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1779|1779]] [[:en:December 28|December 28]]） – Hiraga Gennai was imprisoned on suspicion of accidentally killing two of his disciples. * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] [[:en:December 19|December 19]]） – The Tokugawa shogunate signed the "Yokohama Residence Memorandum" with Britain, the United States, France, and the Netherlands.
== Births == * [[:en:En'ō|En'ō]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1239|1239]] [[:en:December 17|December 17]]） – Fujiwara Yoritsugu, the 5th shogun of the Kamakura shogunate (d. [[:en:1256|1256]]). * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 18th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1733|1733]] [[:en:December 26|December 26]]） – Makino Sadanaga, a politician (d. [[:en:1796|1796]]). * {{ill|Enkyō|1=Enkyō|2=ja|3=延享|lt=Enkyō|ja|Enkyō|1=Enkyō (Edo period)|2=ja|3=延享|lt=Enkyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1183728}} 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1747|1747]] [[:en:December 17|December 17]]） – Arakida Hisao, a kokugaku scholar (d. [[:en:1804|1804]]). * [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1863|1863]] [[:en:December 31|December 31]]） – Nagase Tomorō, the founder of Kao (d. [[:en:1911|1911]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1871|1871]] [[:en:January 11|January 11]]） – Taoka Reiun, a writer and critic (d. [[:en:1912|1912]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei]] 13th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1481|1481]] [[:en:December 12|December 12]]） – Ikkyū Sōjun, a Zen monk (born [[:en:1394|1394]]). * [[:en:Man'en|Man'en]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1861|1861]] [[:en:January 1|January 1]]） – Asaka Gensai, a Confucian scholar ({{ill|Ru (Confucian scholar)|ja|儒学者|zh|儒生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ru (Confucian scholar)|qq=}}) (born [[:en:1791|1791]]).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|20|11|22|10|21|12|11|1121|qq=}}
==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}
[[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 21 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月廿一]]
{{translated page|ja|11月21日 (旧暦)|version=60216887|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[cdo:11 nguŏk 21 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[de:November 21 (Lunisolar)]]
[[ko:음력 11월 21일]] [[ja:11月21日 (旧暦)]] [[zh:十一月廿一]]
[[ar:November 21 (Lunisolar)]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 21 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月廿一]] <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 21 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:十一月廿一]] {{wikidata link|Q841085}} [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Days 21 of Japanese Lunar months]] [[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] [[Category:Lunisolar November]] [[Category:Senshō]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 21 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月21日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 21일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 21 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月廿一]][[zh:十一月廿一]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 21 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月21日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 21일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 21 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月廿一]][[zh:十一月廿一]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:11 nguŏk 21 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:11月21日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 11월 21일]][[zh-min-nan:11 goe̍h 21 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:十一月廿一]][[zh:十一月廿一]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q841085]] Wikidata name: 21st day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|November 21|qq=November 21}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=十一月廿一|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 11월 21일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar November|qq=}} '''November 21''' is the 21st day of the month {{ill|November (Lunisolar)|ja|11月 (旧暦)|lt=November (Shimotsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 11th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senshū|WD=Q96759839}}{{efn|ja|先勝|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Ninna|en|Ninna|WD=Q1346317|lt=Ninna}} 3rd year（{{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|887|en|887|WD=Q25010|lt=887}} {{ill|December 9|en|December 9|WD=Q2304|lt=December 9}}） – The Daijō-daijin and Fujiwara Mototsune were appointed as {{ill|Kanpaku|ja|関白|fr|Kanpaku|ru|Кампаку|sv|Kampaku|uk|Кампаку|zh|關白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpaku|1=kampaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1199966}}, becoming the first commoner Kanpaku. * {{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|WD=Q1327340|lt=Tenkei}} 2nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|940|en|940|WD=Q23830|lt=940}} {{ill|January 3|en|January 3|WD=Q2156|lt=January 3}}） – Taira no Masakado attacked the Hitachi provincial government, seizing official seals and capturing Hitachi-no-kasuke and Fujiwara Noriaki. * [[:en:承徳|承徳]] 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1097|en|1097|WD=Q19488|lt=1097}} {{ill|December 27|en|December 27|WD=Q2800|lt=December 27}}） – Due to a celestial event, the era name was changed from Eichō to Shōtoku. * [[乾元 (日本)|乾元]] 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1302|en|1302|WD=Q5612|lt=1302}} {{ill|December 10|en|December 10|WD=Q2302|lt=December 10}}） – The era name was changed from Seian to Kangen for the accession of Emperor Go-Nijō. * [[:en:安永|安永]] 8th year（{{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1779|en|1779|WD=Q7714|lt=1779}} {{ill|December 28|en|December 28|WD=Q2832|lt=December 28}}） – Hiraga Gennai was imprisoned on suspicion of accidentally killing two of his disciples. * {{ill|Genji era|en|Genji era|WD=Q1325419|lt=Genji}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1864|en|1864|WD=Q7704|lt=1864}} {{ill|December 19|en|December 19|WD=Q2451|lt=December 19}}） – The Tokugawa shogunate signed the "Yokohama Residence Memorandum" with Britain, the United States, France, and the Netherlands.
== Births == * {{ill|En'ō|en|En'ō|WD=Q1193942|lt=En'ō}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1239|en|1239|WD=Q5434|lt=1239}} {{ill|December 17|en|December 17|WD=Q2413|lt=December 17}}） – Fujiwara Yoritsugu, the 5th shogun of the Kamakura shogunate (d. {{ill|1256|en|1256|WD=Q5502|lt=1256}}). * {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō}} 18th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1733|en|1733|WD=Q6972|lt=1733}} {{ill|December 26|en|December 26|WD=Q2761|lt=December 26}}） – Makino Sadanaga, a politician (d. {{ill|1796|en|1796|WD=Q7818|lt=1796}}). * {{ill|Enkyō|1=Enkyō|2=ja|3=延享|lt=Enkyō|ja|Enkyō|1=Enkyō (Edo period)|2=ja|3=延享|lt=Enkyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1183728}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1747|en|1747|WD=Q7596|lt=1747}} {{ill|December 17|en|December 17|WD=Q2413|lt=December 17}}） – Arakida Hisao, a kokugaku scholar (d. {{ill|1804|en|1804|WD=Q6894|lt=1804}}). * {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1863|en|1863|WD=Q7701|lt=1863}} {{ill|December 31|en|December 31|WD=Q2912|lt=December 31}}） – Nagase Tomorō, the founder of Kao (d. {{ill|1911|en|1911|WD=Q2076|lt=1911}}). * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}} {{ill|January 11|en|January 11|WD=Q2247|lt=January 11}}） – Taoka Reiun, a writer and critic (d. {{ill|1912|en|1912|WD=Q2077|lt=1912}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Bunmei|en|Bunmei|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei}} 13th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1481|en|1481|WD=Q6631|lt=1481}} {{ill|December 12|en|December 12|WD=Q2369|lt=December 12}}） – Ikkyū Sōjun, a Zen monk (born {{ill|1394|en|1394|WD=Q6398|lt=1394}}). * {{ill|Man'en|en|Man'en|WD=Q1193862|lt=Man'en}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1861|en|1861|WD=Q7693|lt=1861}} {{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1}}） – Asaka Gensai, a Confucian scholar ({{ill|Ru (Confucian scholar)|ja|儒学者|zh|儒生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ru (Confucian scholar)|qq=}}) (born {{ill|1791|en|1791|WD=Q7784|lt=1791}}).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|11|20|11|22|10|21|12|11|1121|qq=}}

== Events == * {{ill|Ninna|en|Ninna|WD=Q1346317|lt=Ninna|1=Ninna}} 3rd year（{{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|887|en|887|WD=Q25010|lt=887|1=887}} {{ill|December 9|en|December 9|WD=Q2304|lt=December 9|1=December 9}}） – The Daijō-daijin and Fujiwara Mototsune were appointed as {{ill|Kanpaku|ja|関白|fr|Kanpaku|ru|Кампаку|sv|Kampaku|uk|Кампаку|zh|關白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpaku|1=kampaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1199966}}, becoming the first commoner Kanpaku. * {{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|WD=Q1327340|lt=Tenkei|1=Tengyō}} 2nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|940|en|940|WD=Q23830|lt=940|1=940}} {{ill|January 3|en|January 3|WD=Q2156|lt=January 3|1=January 3}}） – Taira no Masakado attacked the Hitachi provincial government, seizing official seals and capturing Hitachi-no-kasuke and Fujiwara Noriaki. * [[:en:承徳|承徳]] 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1097|en|1097|WD=Q19488|lt=1097|1=1097}} {{ill|December 27|en|December 27|WD=Q2800|lt=December 27|1=December 27}}） – Due to a celestial event, the era name was changed from Eichō to Shōtoku. * [[乾元 (日本)|乾元]] 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1302|en|1302|WD=Q5612|lt=1302|1=1302}} {{ill|December 10|en|December 10|WD=Q2302|lt=December 10|1=December 10}}） – The era name was changed from Seian to Kangen for the accession of Emperor Go-Nijō. * [[:en:安永|安永]] 8th year（{{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1779|en|1779|WD=Q7714|lt=1779|1=1779}} {{ill|December 28|en|December 28|WD=Q2832|lt=December 28|1=December 28}}） – Hiraga Gennai was imprisoned on suspicion of accidentally killing two of his disciples. * {{ill|Genji era|en|Genji era|WD=Q1325419|lt=Genji|1=Genji era}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1864|en|1864|WD=Q7704|lt=1864|1=1864}} {{ill|December 19|en|December 19|WD=Q2451|lt=December 19|1=December 19}}） – The Tokugawa shogunate signed the "Yokohama Residence Memorandum" with Britain, the United States, France, and the Netherlands.
== Births == * {{ill|En'ō|en|En'ō|WD=Q1193942|lt=En'ō|1=En'ō}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} a kokugaku scholar (d. {{ill|1804|en|1804|WD=Q6894|lt=1804|1=1804}}). * {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū|1=Bunkyū}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871|1=1871}} {{ill|January 11|en|January 11|WD=Q2247|lt=January 11|1=January 11}}） – Taoka Reiun, a
